title: Hay — there is / there are
objectives: Use 'hay' to say what exists in a place
Distinguish 'hay' from 'estar' (location of a known thing)

vocab.hint: single form for both singular and plural

grammar_notes: 'hay' = 'there is/are'. Used to introduce something NEW or unknown into the conversation.
'está/están' = location of something ALREADY KNOWN. Compare: Hay un libro en la mesa. → El libro está en la mesa.
Russian has no perfect parallel — closest is 'есть' (existence) vs 'находится' (location).
example_phrases: Hay un perro en el parque. (There's a dog in the park.)
En mi clase hay veinte estudiantes.
